item.text: I’m reading `project/src/hello.js` first, then I’ll explain its behavior and point out one concrete improvement.

`[hello.js](/data/data/com.termux/files/home/codex-test-workspace/project/src/hello.js)` contains a single statement:

```js
console.log("Hello");
```

It writes `Hello` to standard output when the file is executed, so running it with Node will print that text to the terminal.

One improvement: make the message configurable instead of hardcoded, for example by exporting a function like `sayHello(name)` or reading from a variable. That makes the file reusable instead of only useful for one fixed string.
